reasoning: Multiple lines of evidence indicate very low probability of a North American country receiving a CDC yellow fever travel advisory by June 1, 2025: (1) Yellow fever circulation ends north of Panama with Mexico declared YF-free since 1963 (search_3_summary); (2) No yellow fever cases reported in Caribbean islands in recent history (search_4_result_5); (3) All 2025 cases concentrated in South America (Guyana, Argentina, Paraguay projections) with no North American spread indicated (search_0_summary, search_3_summary); (4) Historical base rate of YF advisories for North America is extremely low. Reduced from 18% to 12% based on strong geographic/epidemiological evidence.
